name: Satakunta (Finland)

colors.symbolism: the gules field of the historic Satakunta arms
the golden charges of the arms

description: A rectangular red flag charged centrally with the gold provincial arms of Satakunta, depicting a crowned bear-and-sword device on the red field.
history: Satakunta became a region under Finland's 1997 administrative reform; its banner is the heraldic flag of the regional coat of arms, derived from the historic arms of the Satakunta province documented from the late sixteenth century.

features.symbolism: the historic provincial arms of Satakunta: a black bear holding a crowned sword, recalling the medieval province on the western Finnish coast.
